Transcrição Completa: 35 Result Driven Services

 

 

[00:00]"[music]"

 

[00:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Result Driven Services"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Título principal do vídeo, introdução ao conceito de serviços orientados a resultados vs. tempo e esforço

 

[00:05]"Income potential in your personal life and in your business is directly attributed to if you sell result driven services or if you sell time and effort driven intensive services."

 

[00:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Personal Trainer Example - Time & Effort Model"

 

NÚMEROS: $150-$200/sessão (padrão), $500/sessão (high-ticket/celebridades)

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de personal trainer, modelo de precificação por hora/sessão, limitação de escalabilidade baseada em tempo disponível

 

[00:20]"Let's use the examples of something other than an agency to start. If you're a personal trainer, let's say you get paid $150 to $200 a session. Maybe you're an extremely high ticket one that works with celebrities and you get paid $500 a session."

 

[00:38]"Let's say it's just you doing it. Maybe even you have one business partner or another trainer underneath you that you are paying to also help you expand your business means you are going to be limited to the amount of money that that person could make individually and that they could make as a business"

 

[00:58]"because the amount of money that can be made is based on the amount of time and effort that is sold to make it. It's simply the hour that they're paying for as the client in that example. It is a limited financial business. You see what I'm saying?"

 

[01:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Personal Trainer - Result Driven Model"

 

NÚMEROS: $5K (abs em 30 dias), $10K (corpo desejado em 60 dias)

 

VISUAL: Transformação de modelo de precificação: sessões por hora → resultado específico em prazo definido, comparação visual de modelos de negócio

 

[01:16]"When you sell results, on the other hand, let's say that the personal trainer says to you, instead of saying, 'Okay, it's 150 a session or $500 a session,' let's say instead they say, 'If I get you abs in 30 days, you're going to pay me 5K.'"

 

[01:33]"Or let's say the personal trainer says, 'If I get you the body that you're looking for, and here's a visual of what we're talking about so we can agree on what that would look like in 60 days. Pay me 10K.'"

 

[01:45]"That'd be an example of a result-driven service rather than selling the time and effort."

 

[01:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Chef/Meal Prep Example - Time Limitation"

 

NÚMEROS: $3,500/mês, 2x por semana (segunda e quinta/quarta), 4-8 horas por dia de trabalho, 3-4 clientes máximo

 

VISUAL: Caso real de chef de meal prep, estrutura de trabalho semanal, limitação de clientes devido a tempo disponível, modelo de expansão limitado

 

[01:52]"Let's talk about chefs. So, here you uh in my house, we have meal prep with a chef, but it costs about $3,500 a month. Okay?"

 

[02:02]"So, the chef, she was talking to us about this. She has about three, four different clients that she can do this with. She comes twice a week. So, she'll come on a Monday and she'll come on a Thursday usually, sometimes on a Wednesday."

 

[02:16]"She on the other hours of the day, it takes her like four to eight hours to cook depending on the day. And she does that twice a week."

 

[02:24]"She could maybe go to another client's house in the same day. And all in all, she only has the other days of the week available in order for her to do the same thing and expand the amount of financial capital she's bringing in to her business"

 

[02:38]"without having to hire somebody else and then put all the time, energy, and attention that's involved in the expenses in expanding the amount of leads that can come to that new person that's been hired in."

 

[02:49]"Same kind of concept. In order to expand there, it still takes time and effort. And in order for her to grow her business, she'd have to take on more clients, but she's selling time and effort."

 

[03:00]"So once again, still not going to be a good business model. It's going to financially limit you."

 

[03:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Maid Service Example - Time & Effort Trap"

 

NÚMEROS: $200 por limpeza, 3-4 horas por casa, 3-4 meses para expansão

 

VISUAL: Serviço de limpeza doméstica, modelo de precificação por tempo, problema de escalabilidade através de contratação, dono vira "babá glorificada"

 

[03:07]"Let's talk about a maid. Somebody who comes into your home, you pay them $200 to clean your house and it takes three to four hours."

 

[03:15]"You get my point I'm making here. They can only sell so many people in a week because there's only as many maids as they have in their business. They have to hire more maids in order to expand."

 

[03:27]"And after, let's say, three, four months of expanding, the person who owns the business is going to be a glorified babysitter."

 

[03:34]"They're going to be babysitting all the maids. They're going to be babysitting all the clients and on top of that they're going to have the responsibilities of generating leads for the people that they're closing to bring in more capital in the business since that's the type of business that they chose to scale with."

 

[03:49]"Financially limiting and very time and effort intensive."

 

[03:53] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Agency Services - Time vs. Results"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Transição para contexto de agência, todos os serviços podem ser vendidos de duas formas: tempo/esforço ou resultados

 

[03:55]"So inside of your business when you hear me talk about all these different services, you can sell every service that I've talked about with time and effort instead of selling it with results. Everything."

 

[04:07] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Photography/Video - Time Model vs. Result Model"

 

NÚMEROS: $500-$1,000/shoot (tempo e esforço), $10K (vídeo de resultado/marca de 60 segundos-2 minutos)

 

VISUAL: Comparação de modelos de precificação em fotografia/vídeo: custo por shoot vs. vídeo de marca de alta qualidade, outsourcing de edição

 

[04:09]"If I'm a photographer or a video person, if I sell you on a cost per shoot, that might be $500 or $1,000, but that is time and effort."

 

[04:19]"However, if I sell you on a result, let's say a 60-second to two minute video where it's a glorified personal brand video or a glorified business video where I might come on site for a few hours, maybe I outsource the editing to somebody else."

 

[04:35]"It's really not about the time and effort involved in that example, but you're going to pay me 10K for the result of that quality video compared to what everybody else you could have paid would have been."

 

[04:47] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Buyer Psychology - Price vs. Value"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Psicologia do comprador: investimento baseado em probabilidade de retorno vs. preço mais baixo, relação entre preço alto e confiança em resultado

 

[04:49]"Now, the person who's spending money there, so you don't project your own values onto this and misinterpret what I'm saying. There's all kinds of people that are willing to spend more money for a result than they are simply to get a cheaper deal."

 

[05:04]"You see, people usually will not invest money into things that they're desiring a result for for the cheapest thing that's available."

 

[05:13]"They'll invest into what they have the highest probability to get a return on the investment and the energy put into it which is like most likely to be the most expensive option especially if they have it within their financial means."

 

[05:27]"It's whoever is selling them with certainty and confidence that they can do what they are selling and what the person is desiring as an out as as an outcome. Okay."

 

[05:39] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Social Media Marketing - Wrong Approach"

 

NÚMEROS: 2x por semana ou 1x por semana (frequência de reporting)

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de serviço errado: criação de conteúdo + postagem diária + reporting sem distribuição paga, ausência de resultado financeiro mensurável

 

[05:41]"So, if you're positioned right now selling, let's say, social media marketing, you're creating content, you're posting content every day, and on top of that, you're reporting twice a week or once a week to the person,"

 

[05:54]"you're selling a time inensive and an effort inensive service that does not have a financial means to the end, unless you are also paying to distribute that content and handling the advertising in order to monetize the content that's being distributed."

 

[06:12]"posting content, not distributing it, and not running ads is not a result-driven service because there's no result to be made on organic content that's posted."

 

[06:24]"Especially nowadays when you're posting without paid distribution behind it."

 

[06:29] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Identifying Result-Driven Services"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Critério de identificação: foco em resultado financeiro ou outcome específico vs. atividades e tarefas

 

[06:31]"To be very clear, it's really easy to identify because you're talking about a financial means or an outcome when you're selling results."

 

[06:40] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Email Marketing - Result-Driven Model"

 

NÚMEROS: 50,000 pessoas na lista, $10K base, +$100K/mês resultado = $20K pagamento, $30K para resultados superiores

 

VISUAL: Caso de email marketing orientado a resultados: cliente com lista existente, reescrita de emails, melhorias em open rates e click-through rates, estrutura de pagamento escalonada baseada em resultados financeiros

 

[06:42]"If I sell email marketing, let's say that somebody already has a list of 50,000 people. They already have some great funnels. They even already have a paid traffic guy or girl. They don't need somebody who's doing ads."

 

[06:56]"They want somebody who could come in and help get them higher open rates, higher click-through rates, and higher amounts of sales from the email list."

 

[07:04]"You could say, 'Hey, listen. For 10K, we're going to come in and rewrite every email that you have, and we are going to work with your email provider with a few things that we know we can do because of our ability to take previous knowhow and apply it to this situation."

 

[07:21]"And we're going to get your open rates up. We're going to get your click-through rates up. We don't know exactly what they're going to be, but here's the thing."

 

[07:28]"If we make you an extra 100k a month based on what we do, pay us 20K instead of 10K."

 

[07:34]"If we just do what we do and it gets you additional results, but it doesn't get you to that benchmark, that's great. Just stick with the 10K instead."

 

[07:42]"If we make you this much more, pay us 30K instead."

 

[07:46]"And long story short, the person who's sitting there saying, 'Okay, am I going to make this investment or not?' They already know they want it. You're talking about outcomes. You're talking about results."

 

[07:57]"Why would the person disagree to paying you a larger sum of money when they're making a larger sum of money from your work? They wouldn't."

 

[08:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Facebook Advertising - Wrong vs. Right Approach"

 

NÚMEROS: 100 ads mínimo, 2 horas/dia de trabalho, 1x por semana reporting (abordagem errada), 3x leads atuais, 3-5x receita adicional, 10x leads = 10x receita (abordagem correta), $10K base, $20K (3-5x), $30K (10x)

 

VISUAL: Comparação de duas abordagens de venda de Facebook Ads: foco em atividades (errado) vs. foco em multiplicação de leads e receita (correto), estrutura de pagamento escalonada

 

[08:07]"You could do the same thing with advertising, of course. You could say, 'Well, when we do Facebook advertising, we are going to make sure that we launch at least 100 ads and we are going to make sure we work on your account at least two hours a day, and we're going to make sure we have reporting to you at least once a week.'"

 

[08:26]"That specific way to sell Facebook advertising would be the wrong way to do it."

 

[08:32]"Instead, you would say, 'We're going to drive you 3x the amount of leads that you're getting right now. We're going to use Facebook to do it.'"

 

[08:40]"That should result in about three to 5x the additional revenue to your business. If we make that happen for you, instead of paying us 10K, pay us 20K instead."

 

[08:50]"Or if we make you 10x the amount of leads that you're getting right now, and that results in 10x the amount of revenue, why don't you go ahead and pay us 30K instead?"

 

[09:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Core Principle - Same Service, Different Approach"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Síntese final: todos os serviços podem ser vendidos de duas formas (tempo/esforço vs. resultados), escolha determina potencial de receita

 

[09:02]"So my point is, when you're selling result driven services, instead of menial tasks that require time and effort, the same exact things can be sold, but from two different approaches and angles."

 

[09:14]"You hear me talk about this all the time. Make sure you sell result driven services. Do not sell time and effort."

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Personal Trainer (tempo/esforço): $150-$200/sessão padrão, $500/sessão high-ticket celebridades

Personal Trainer (resultado): $5K (abs em 30 dias), $10K (corpo desejado em 60 dias)

Chef/Meal Prep: $3,500/mês, 2x semana, 4-8 horas/dia, máximo 3-4 clientes

Maid Service: $200/limpeza, 3-4 horas/casa, 3-4 meses para expandir estrutura

Fotografia/Vídeo (tempo): $500-$1,000/shoot

Fotografia/Vídeo (resultado): $10K vídeo de marca 60 segundos-2 minutos

Email Marketing: Lista de 50,000 pessoas, $10K base, $20K (+$100K/mês resultado), $30K (resultados superiores)

Facebook Ads (errado): 100 ads mínimo, 2 horas/dia, 1x/semana reporting

Facebook Ads (correto): 3x leads atuais → 3-5x receita ($20K), 10x leads → 10x receita ($30K), base $10K

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Result-Driven vs. Time-Driven Philosophy: Princípio fundamental que determina potencial de renda pessoal e empresarial

Financial Limitation Recognition: Negócios baseados em tempo/esforço são financeiramente limitados pela disponibilidade de horas

Scalability Through Results: Vender resultados permite crescimento sem proporcional aumento de tempo investido

Outcome-Based Pricing: Precificação baseada em resultado específico alcançado, não em horas trabalhadas

Visual Agreement Protocol: Estabelecer acordo visual sobre resultado desejado antes de começar (ex: corpo desejado em 60 dias)

Tiered Payment Structure: Sistema de pagamento escalonado baseado em níveis de resultado alcançados

Buyer Psychology Leverage: Pessoas investem mais em alta probabilidade de retorno do que em preço baixo

Certainty & Confidence Selling: Vender com certeza e confiança sobre capacidade de entregar resultado

Glorified Babysitter Trap: Expansão através de contratação em modelo tempo/esforço transforma dono em "babá" de equipe

Lead Generation Burden: Modelos escalados por contratação criam responsabilidade adicional de gerar leads para novos contratados

Outsourcing for Efficiency: Terceirizar tarefas operacionais (ex: edição) para focar em entrega de resultado

Paid Distribution Requirement: Conteúdo orgânico sem distribuição paga não é serviço orientado a resultado

Financial Means Identification: Serviços orientados a resultado sempre falam sobre resultado financeiro ou outcome específico

Benchmark-Based Escalation: Estabelecer benchmarks claros que acionam aumentos de pagamento

Existing Infrastructure Leverage: Trabalhar com clientes que já têm assets (lista, funnels, tráfego) para focar em otimização

Multiplier Language: Falar em termos de multiplicadores (3x, 5x, 10x) em vez de atividades executadas

Same Service, Two Angles: Todo serviço pode ser vendido como tempo/esforço OU como resultado - escolha determina sucesso

 

ESTRUTURA:

Income Potential Framework: Renda diretamente atribuída a tipo de serviço vendido (resultado vs. tempo/esforço)

Business Model Comparison Structure:

Personal Trainer: sessões → resultado de transformação física

Chef: horas de cozinha → programa de nutrição/resultado

Maid: horas de limpeza → padrão de limpeza mantido

Fotografia/Vídeo: custo por shoot → vídeo de marca de qualidade

Social Media: postagens diárias → monetização via ads

Email Marketing: atividade de reescrita → aumento de vendas mensuráveis

Facebook Ads: número de ads/horas → multiplicação de leads/receita

Three-Tier Result Pricing Model:

Tier 1: Base fee ($10K) - resultado padrão alcançado

Tier 2: Premium fee ($20K) - benchmark intermediário alcançado

Tier 3: Elite fee ($30K) - benchmark superior alcançado

Expansion Problem in Time-Based Models:

Limitação de horas disponíveis

Necessidade de contratar para crescer

Dono vira gerente/babá de equipe

Adiciona responsabilidade de geração de leads

Aumenta complexidade sem proporcional aumento de lucro

Result-Driven Service Identification Checklist:

✓ Fala sobre resultado financeiro específico

✓ Define outcome mensurável

✓ Não menciona horas trabalhadas como valor

✓ Usa linguagem de multiplicadores (3x, 5x, 10x)

✓ Estabelece benchmarks de performance

✓ Inclui estrutura de pagamento escalonada

Wrong Approach Red Flags:

Mencionar quantidade de ads criados

Prometer horas de trabalho por dia

Focar em frequência de reporting

Vender criação de conteúdo sem distribuição

Precificar por sessão/hora/dia

Não conectar atividade a resultado financeiro

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Modelo Personal Trainer tradicional: $150-$200/sessão = renda limitada por horas disponíveis

Modelo Personal Trainer resultado: $5K-$10K por transformação = desacoplado de tempo

Chef meal prep: $3,500/mês × 3-4 clientes máximo = teto de $14K/mês sem contratar

Maid service: $200/casa × limitado por horas disponíveis = escala apenas via contratação

Vídeo/foto tradicional: $500-$1,000/shoot = renda por projeto limitada

Vídeo de marca resultado: $10K = 10-20x mais que modelo tradicional

Email marketing escalonado: $10K base → $20K (+$100K/mês cliente) → $30K (resultados superiores)

Facebook Ads resultado: $10K base → $20K (3-5x receita) → $30K (10x receita)

ROI para cliente em modelo resultado: Multiplicadores de 3x-10x em leads e receita

Custo de expansão em modelo tempo/esforço: Salários + treinamento + geração de leads + gerenciamento

Margem em modelo resultado: Alta (não proporcional a horas trabalhadas)

Margem em modelo tempo/esforço: Limitada (diretamente proporcional a horas disponíveis)

Valor percebido: Preço alto = maior confiança em resultado (psicologia do comprador)

Lifetime value: Clientes de resultado pagam mais e renovam mais (focados em outcome, não em custo)

 

DURAÇÃO: 09:24 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional/treinamento de agência, apresentação conceitual com múltiplos exemplos práticos
